Skip to contents
aquacroptools 0.1.0
  • Reference

aquacroptools

aquacroptools is a set of tools specifically developed to work with AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) files. AquaCrop is a crop-water productivity model developed by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations. It is used to simulate and evaluate the response of various crops to water availability and provides insights into crop water requirements, yield potential, and water productivity.

aquacroptools is designed to make Aquacrop v7.0 files handling much easier and allow users to programmatically :

  1. Generate AquaCrop v7.0 files.

  2. Read,manipulate Aquacrop v7.0 files.

Installation

You can install the development version of aquacroptools from GitHub with:

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("oousmane/aquacroptools")

Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to use aquacroptools:

library(aquacroptools)
library(fs)
## basic example code
data("weather") # load example dataset

head(weather) # check the data sets structure. The column names should be as is.

if(!dir_ls("climate/")) dir_create("climate/") # output directories

write_plu(path = "climate/") # write .PLU file
write_tnx(path = "climate/") # write .Tnx file
write_et0(path = "climate/") # write .ETo file
write_cli(
  path = "climate/", #path were we have <<station>>.* (PLU,ETo, Tnx)
  stn ="wakanda", # name of the station <<station>>.* (PLU,ETo, Tnx)
  scenario ="hist" # climate data scenario : hist, rcpxx or sspxxx,
  check_files = TRUE
  )
